page: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 128 vCPUs and 1952.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz, the architecture comprises 64 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU allocation is dedicated, supported by a 54 MB L3 cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves a stress-ng multi-core score of 120,736.75 and a PassMark CPU score of 81,431.63, both placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 6,507.69 GB/sec and large block uncached speeds at 298.49 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in cryptographic operations, scoring 104,535.88 on the PassMark CPU encryption test, and ray tracing, scoring 105,464.0 on Geekbench.

While multi-core and memory-bound operations are highly performant, single-core performance is moderate. The stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1,843.97 and Geekbench single-core score of 1,578.0 fall into the average category. Additionally, while prompt processing for small and medium LLM models is strong, text generation for large 70B models is weak, yielding only 2.73 tokens/sec. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of memory relative to CPU cores makes it a specialized resource allocation, reducing the need for multi-node clustering for large in-memory datasets.

This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy database operations, as evidenced by its PassMark database score of 28,527.39 and Redis SET throughput of 106.54 million operations per second. It is also applicable for multi-threaded software compilation, image processing, and static web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server configured with 128 dedicated vCPUs and 1952.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor operating at 2.6 GHz across 64 physical cores, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. It delivers strong multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 120,736.75 and a large-block read bandwidth of 298.49 GB/sec. Cryptographic and ray-tracing capabilities are top-tier, though single-core performance and large-model LLM text generation remain average to weak. Qualitatively, the instance offers high memory density per core, making it highly efficient for consolidating massive datasets without clustering. It is best suited for large in-memory databases, Redis deployments, enterprise applications, and intensive database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs and 1952 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for large-scale database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-128 is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 1952 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring 1952 GB RAM and 128 dedicated vCPUs for massive in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs and 1952.0 GB RAM powered by Intel Xeon processors.
Strong multi-core performance with a PassMark CPU score of 81,431.63.
High-speed memory bandwidth reaching 298.49 GB/sec for large uncached blocks.
Top-tier cryptographic and ray-tracing benchmark results.
Average single-core performance and weak large-model LLM text generation.
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis, and heavy database operations.
